visual_checks: Check all normal-sized text (below 18pt or 14pt bold) for a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 against its background
Check all large text (18pt+ regular or 14pt+ bold) for a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background
Look for text over images or gradients where contrast may vary across the text
Check placeholder text in form fields for sufficient contrast
Look for light gray text on white backgrounds or dark gray text on black backgrounds that may fail enhanced contrast
Verify that text in hover states, focus states, and active states also meets enhanced contrast requirements
pass_conditions: All normal-sized text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 against its background
All large text (18pt+ regular or 14pt+ bold) has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background
Text over images or gradients maintains the required contrast ratio across all portions of the text
Placeholder text and form labels meet the enhanced contrast requirements
Interactive state text (hover, focus, active) meets the enhanced contrast requirements
fail_conditions: Normal-sized text has a contrast ratio below 7:1 against its background
Large text has a contrast ratio below 4.5:1 against its background
Text placed over images or gradients drops below the required contrast in some areas
Placeholder text or form labels have insufficient contrast (e.g., light gray on white)
Text in hover, focus, or active states fails to meet enhanced contrast ratios
na_conditions: Page contains no text content (unlikely but possible for purely decorative pages)
Text is part of a logo or brand name where no contrast requirement applies
Text is purely decorative and conveys no information
Text is part of an inactive or disabled UI component
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Page styles failed to load so actual text colors cannot be determined
common_mistakes: Meeting the AA 4.5:1 standard but not the enhanced AAA 7:1 ratio for normal text
Using mid-gray text (#767676 or lighter) on white that passes AA but fails AAA
Text over photographic backgrounds where contrast varies depending on the image region
Placeholder text with very low contrast assumed to be exempt from requirements
Ignoring contrast of text in interactive states (links on hover, focused button text)
Assuming large text thresholds when the actual rendered font size is below 18pt/14pt bold

examples.pass: Body text is #000000 on #FFFFFF (21:1 ratio) and headings are #1a1a1a on #FFFFFF (17.4:1 ratio), both exceeding the 7:1 enhanced requirement
examples.fail: Body text is #767676 on #FFFFFF (4.54:1 ratio) which passes AA but fails the AAA enhanced requirement of 7:1
examples.partial: Most text on the page meets 7:1 contrast, but navigation links at #595959 on #FFFFFF (5.9:1) and footer text at #888888 on #F5F5F5 (3.5:1) fall below the enhanced thresholds

auditor_anti_patterns: FALSE POSITIVE: flagging contrast/color of elements that are not visible to sighted users. Bootstrap '.visually-hidden' / '.sr-only' (and the older '.screen-reader-text', '.visuallyhidden') are CSS utility classes that clip the element to a 1px box and remove it from the visual layout while leaving it in the accessibility tree. The element is intentionally only readable by screen readers -- it is not rendered to the screen at all -- so visual rules like color contrast, font size, and luminance ratios DO NOT APPLY. Before flagging text or a control for contrast, check whether the element (or any ancestor) carries one of these utility classes or an equivalent clip:rect(0,0,0,0)/position:absolute/width:1px/height:1px pattern. If it does, do NOT report a contrast finding -- there is no rendered pixel to measure.
plain_meaning: A stricter version of 1.4.3: text and images of text must have a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 (4.5:1 for large text). This AAA threshold targets users with more significant low vision. Logotypes, incidental text, and disabled controls remain exempt.
scope_applies_to: Body text, headings, labels, and paragraph copy in their rendered state
Button text and link text in their normal state
Images of text in the informational content
Placeholder text when used to convey information
scope_does_not_apply_to: Elements removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden, display:none, visibility:hidden, hidden attribute)
Visually-hidden / sr-only text clipped to a 1px box (no rendered glyphs to measure)
Disabled / inactive form controls and buttons (explicitly exempt)
Incidental text in photographs or background imagery
Content not yet visible (hover/focus reveals evaluated in their visible state)
borderline_calibration: Measured ratios of 7:1 or higher for normal text PASS.
Large text threshold is 4.5:1; treat it as a hard line.
Do NOT flag sr-only / visually-hidden text as AAA contrast failures.
This is a Level AAA criterion; apply it only when AAA evaluation is requested or when 1.4.3 already passes and stricter review is appropriate.
Borderline results within 0.1 of the threshold should be reported with reduced severity.
